data.title: China hits 20 U.S. firms with sanctions over Taiwan arms sales
data.description:
China's foreign ministry said Friday it is imposing sanctions targeting 20 U.S. defense-related companies and 10 executives, a week after the Trump administration announced more than $11 billion in arms sales to Taiwan.

The sanctioned companies include Northrop Grumman Systems Corp. (NOC), L3Harris Maritime Services (LHX), and Boeing's (BA) operations in St. Louis, Missouri.

The measures freeze any assets the companies and individuals hold in China and bar domestic organizations and individuals from doing business with them, and the individuals on the list, including the founder of Anduril Industries and nine senior executives from the sanctioned firms, are banned from entering China, the ministry said.

The actual impact of the measures likely will be minimal, since most of the companies and executives targeted have little or no presence in China, and some already were banned from entry by China's commerce ministry.

"The Taiwan question is at the very core of China's interests and the first red line that cannot be crossed in China-U.S. relations," the Chinese foreign ministry said in a statement.

data.title: Foreign phone shipments in China surge 128% in November: report
data.description:
Shipments of foreign-branded phones, including Apple's (AAPL) iPhone, in China surged 128.4% in November year-over-year, Reuters reported, citing its calculations based on data from a government-affiliated research firm.

The data from the China Academy of Information and Communications Technology, or CAICT, showed that overall phone shipments in China were up 1.9% year-over-year to 30.16 million units in November. Shipments of foreign-branded phones were 6.93 million units, the report added.

The shipments of foreign-branded phones were 7.027 million units in October 2025, showing growth of 13% year-over-year. 

China's smartphone market is dominated by companies such as vivo, Xiaomi (XIACF) (XIACY), Huawei Technologies, Apple (AAPL), Oppo, and Honor.

data.title: Leifras raises ¥2.5 billion, diversifying fundraising channels
China unveils venture capital funds to back tech startups focused on 'hard technology' - report
data.description:
China has launched three venture capital funds to invest in "hard technology" areas, Reuters reported, citing state broadcaster CCTV.

The plans for capital contribution in the funds have been finalized, each with over 50B yuan (about $7.14B), the report added.

The funds will mainly invest in early-stage startups, and the targets should be valued at less than 500M yuan, an official noted, adding that no single investment would amount to over 50M yuan, according to the report.

The funds' anticipated targets include companies focused on integrated circuits, quantum technology, biomedicine, brain-computer interfaces, aerospace, and other important hard technologies. "Soft" technologies would include internet services, the report added.

data.title: Japan and China indexes move higher as sentiment remains elevated
data.description:
Asian stock markets traded mostly higher on Friday, ending the holiday-shortened week on a positive note. Investor sentiment remained elevated amid ongoing geopolitical tensions and expectations of further U.S. interest rate cuts

In metals, gold prices rose to $4,510 per ounce after briefly hitting an all-time high of $4,53, while silver climbed more than 4% to $74.8 per ounce, advancing for the fifth consecutive session to reach a new high.  

Japan (NKY:IND) gained 0.56%, rebounding from three subdued days and heading for a second straight weekly gain. Japan's unemployment rate steady at 2.6% in November, retail sales rise.

Japan’s 10-year government bond yield steadied around 2%, as investors continue to expect possible interest rate hikes from the BOJ despite Tokyo’s inflation coming in below expectations.

China (SHCOMP) rose 0.47%. The offshore yuan appreciated to around 7 per dollar, extending gains for the third consecutive session, as investors responded to signs of measured policy guidance from authorities.

Hong Kong (HSI) closed for Christmas.

In other parts of Asia, Singapore’s manufacturing production grew 14.3% Y/Y in November.

The Philippines' trade deficit narrowed to $3.51 billion in November.

India (SENSEX) fell 0.30%.

Australia (AS51) closed for Christmas.

Wall Street’s major market averages finished higher on Wednesday in the shortened Christmas Eve session, with both the Dow and S&P 500 closing at record highs.

U.S. stock futures were mixed on Friday: Dow -0.10%; S&P 500 +0.02%; Nasdaq +0.08%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

data.title: Japan's unemployment rate steady at 2.6% in November, retail sales rise
data.description:
• Japan’s unemployment rate stood at 2.6% in November, unchanged from the previous three months and in line with estimates. It remained the highest reading since July 2024.
  • Retail sales in Japan rose 1.0% Y/Y in November, slowing from a 1.7% growth in the previous month but surpassing market expectations of 0.9%.It marked the third consecutive month of increase in retail turnover.
  • The industrial production in Japan decreased 2.1% in November over the same month in the previous year, reversing a 1.6% growth in the previous month and marking the first decline in three months.

data.title: Everybody Loves Languages Corp. announces acquisition by ELL Ventures
data.description:
• Everybody Loves Languages (ELL:CA) said on Wednesday that ELLC and ELL Ventures have executed a business combination agreement  that contemplates ELLC and EV entering into a business combination that would result in all the shareholders of ELLC, other than EV, receiving cash for their ELLC shares.
  • The business combination agreement contemplates a transaction pursuant to which all the shareholders of ELLC immediately prior to the closing of the transaction, other than EV, will receive, in exchange for their shares of ELLC, cash consideration of $0.085 per share. 
  • Pursuant to the business combination agreement, ELLC and EV will amalgamate to form a new corporation. 
  • Upon the amalgamation, the shareholders of ELLC, other than EV, will receive one redeemable preferred share in the capital of Amalco, which redeemable preferred share will immediately be redeemed for $0.085 in cash.

data.title: Friday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
4:30 PM Fed Balance Sheet

The Fed's balance sheet is a weekly report presenting a consolidated balance sheet for all 12 Reserve Banks that lists factors supplying reserves into the banking system and factors absorbing reserves from the system.

data.title: The Westaim Corporation announces normal course issuer bid
data.description:
• The Westaim Corporation (WED:CA) said on Wednesday that the TSX Venture Exchange has accepted a notice filed by the company of its intention to make a normal course issuer bid. 
  • In connection with the NCIB, the company entered into an automatic share purchase plan with TD Securities to allow for purchases of its common shares. 
  • In connection with the commencement of the NCIB, the company will terminate its current normal course issuer bid, which commenced on April 1, 2025. 
  • Under the terminated NCIB, as of December 16, 2025, Westaim purchased an aggregate of 336,564 shares. The company has decided to conclude the terminated NCIB early to allow for future normal course issuer bids to commence concurrently with the start of each calendar year. 
  • Westaim has entered into an ASPP with TD to allow for the purchase of shares under the NCIB at times when the company would ordinarily not be permitted to purchase shares due to regulatory restrictions or self-imposed blackout periods.

data.title: Infinitum CFO Michael Wood resigns; Ikay Deol appointed as CFO
data.description:
• Infinitum Copper (INFI:CA) announced on Wednesday the appointment of Ikavinder (Ikay) Deol as Chief Financial Officer and Corporate Secretary, effective December 23, 2025, replacing Michael Wood. 
  • Wood resigned as a director and officer of the company effective December 22, 2025.
  • Deol is a Chartered Professional Accountant who works primarily with publicly listed junior mining companies, providing comprehensive accounting, financial reporting, and regulatory compliance services under IFRS.
